what_it_does: A running, newest-first journal where each numbered 'slice' records a scoped audit finding, how it changed the plan, and what was built and verified.
observations: Each slice follows a recurring shape: title, user/Info direction, root-cause diagnosis, fix/actions, verification, and next/deferred items.

The log covers a wide arc of work: token recalibration, generative core solvers, archetype catalogue, AI registry unification, AtomiCity app, System Map visualisations, Glyphic system, icon library, component layer, and drag-and-drop editor.
It repeatedly invokes 'single-source registries', 'principle gates', 'anti-drift law', and the ritual 'check_design_system clean'.
It records reversals and redesigns openly, e.g. treemap deemed illegible and replaced, file-size lens broken then fixed, radial sunburst re-thought.
Verification is self-reported through DOM probes, harness pages, console checks, and 'console clean' assertions.
Slice numbering becomes inconsistent later: duplicate 'Slice 69' entries, an out-of-order 'Slice 68' after 'Slice 66', and multiple 'Slice 66' variants.
standouts: Slice 78: radial sunburst re-encoded with folder rings, uniform file spokes, size-encoded lengths, and rim labels.
Slice 70: deterministic FLIP morph so layout correctness no longer depends on CSS transitions finishing.
Slice 69: folder-local weight normalization that made file-size lens stable and proportional without collapsing zones.
Slice 11: AtomiCity standalone app with a Vi learning loop that distils feedback into live CV_AI behaviours.
Slice 6–9: unification of all AI calls through the CV_AI capability registry with loud-fail provider resolution.
Slice 1c: a measured finding that flipped the planned action from retuning tokens to migrating old v1 panels.
conventions: Heavy use of ALL CAPS for emphasis and code literals for file paths and API names.
Explicit 'Verified:', 'Console clean', and 'check_design_system clean' as a verification ritual.
Recording of 'NEXT', 'DEFERRED', and 'OPEN' work so future sessions know what is pending.
Citing principle documents like CLAUDE.md, HANDOFF.md, and UNIFICATION.md.
Including user emotional reactions ('furious', 'rightly furious') as part of the decision context.
concerns: The file is extremely long and dense, making topical retrieval hard without search.
Slice numbering is duplicated and out-of-order in places, weakening the chronological contract.
Large passages read like pasted historical narratives, blurring current state from past attempts.
Verification claims are self-reported and not reproducible from the file alone.
The mix of design rationale, code changes, and user emotions may obscure raw technical facts.
notes: A dense, chronological build diary that tries to make every slice's audit, fix, and verification explicit so future sessions do not repeat work.
questions: Is there an enforced schema for a slice entry, or is the format only conventional?
How does this log relate to PROGRESS.md and HANDOFF.md — is it authoritative or a feed into them?
Which slice numbers are canonical given the duplicates and out-of-order entries?
Are 'verified' claims backed by automated tests, harness pages, or manual probes?
What is the precise boundary between 'done', 'deferred', and 'open'?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches the stated purpose as a per-slice audit log, but also carries extensive historical narrative and user-context color beyond a minimal outcome record.
apparent_themes: Audit before action; root-cause over symptom.
Iterative redesign driven by user critique.
intention_signals: To create a durable, searchable memory of why each change was made, what problem it solved, and what remains open, so autonomous agents can continue without contradicting past decisions or re-litigating solved problems.
novelty: The slice format combines user direction, root-cause diagnosis, fix, verification, and forward planning in one log, and treats failed attempts and emotional user feedback as first-class inputs while enforcing single-source registry discipline.
contribution: A structured, time-ordered decision journal that captures rationale, verification, and open work for each build increment, reducing repeated work and contradictions across autonomous sessions.
summary_for_embedding: A newest-first per-slice build log recording audit findings, root-cause plan changes, implemented fixes, verification steps, and deferred next items for a design-system and codebase project.
intention_for_embedding: To serve as the running memory of the build so autonomous sessions can continue without repeating or contradicting prior work.
